tensions.text: cust.operator-large derives its 0.40 buyer-share estimate partly from the Alexander Group 30%-of-orders figure, but that figure measures one supplier's overall data-center-driven order share, not a breakdown by buyer type (operator vs OEM vs distributor); the source excerpt itself is flagged as 'qualitative channel context only,' so it cannot support a quantified 0.40 large-operator channel share.

None of the retrieved sources substantiate the 'hyperscale+colo ≈ 62% of capacity (Synergy)' figure cited in the cust.operator-large derivation, nor do any sources provide percentage splits for operator-mid, OEM/integrator, or distributor channels — all four node values (0.40/0.25/0.22/0.13) remain unsourced internal estimates with no corroborating quantitative evidence found in this search pass.

Channel-partner taxonomies vary by equipment sub-segment: substations are split into OEMs vs TSO/DSO, transformers into OEMs/Distributors/Online Retailers/Electrical Contractors, and rack PDUs into Direct Sales/System integrators — none report share percentages, and the inconsistent taxonomy across equipment types undermines applying a single unified operator/mid/OEM/distributor split (0.40/0.25/0.22/0.13) uniformly across the whole power-equipment category.

absences: No search result provided a percentage breakdown of PDU or power-equipment spend by buyer type (large operator, mid/enterprise, OEM/integrator, distributor) — only qualitative channel-partner category names were found.
No corroboration was found for the 'Synergy hyperscale+colo ≈ 62% of capacity' figure used in the cust.operator-large derivation.

No source quantified what fraction of a supplier's data-center-driven revenue flows through direct large-operator sales versus OEM or distributor channels — the closest figure (30% of orders) measures total DC exposure, not channel mix.
No distributor-specific revenue share (e.g., for master distributors like MaxPower) was found relative to total data center power equipment spend.

No report found that segments the same power-equipment market simultaneously by both data-center-type (hyperscale/colo/enterprise) and channel-partner type with matched percentages, which would be needed to triangulate buyer mix directly.
